iran_nuke.notes: Day 14 evening — FALLING (continued). NEW: Netanyahu confirms Israeli strikes killed top Iranian nuclear scientists. Combined with Isfahan stockpile buried, Iran's nuclear workforce and material base severely degraded. However: enrichment capability at Fordow/unknown sites still active at 60%. IAEA access denied. New signal: nuclear_scientists_killed (-5, knowledge base destroyed). Trump says US will 'hit Iran hard over next week' — could include further nuclear site targeting. Down from 15→13.

iran_conventional.notes: Day 14 evening — RISING. MAJOR ESCALATION: Israel struck near Quds Day rally in Tehran — explosion during state-organized demonstration with thousands present, at least 1 killed (AP/NYT/Al Jazeera/PBS). Israel had warned it would target the area. Trump: 'US will hit Iran hard over next week.' Hegseth: Khamenei 'wounded and likely hiding.' Marines upgraded to 2,500 (+1 ship). Total US casualties: 13 killed (BBC). Combined force struck regime checkpoints in Tehran for first time + Basij/IRGC/LEC targets. White House internal tug-of-war on exit strategy (Reuters). Iran defiant — Hormuz still closed, Khamenei vows to continue. Up from 65→68.

israel_lebanon.notes: Day 14 evening — ESCALATING FAST. Lebanon death toll: 773 killed, 1,933 wounded (Guardian). 100+ children killed. Israel destroying bridges in south Lebanon + dropping leaflets over Beirut threatening 'Gaza-scale devastation' (Al Jazeera). Bloomberg: expanded incursion preparing. Hezbollah vows 'existential' fight. CNN: Israel 'seizes moment to finish Hezbollah.' 2 IDF soldiers killed, 14 wounded. 800K+ displaced. Zone: deep critical. Up from 74→78.

turkey.notes: CRITICAL — RISING. Third Iranian ballistic missile intercepted by NATO over Turkey (Mar 13). Sirens in Adana province near Incirlik Air Base. NATO confirmed intercept 'in eastern Mediterranean.' Turkey formally asking Tehran to clarify, warning against further attacks. Politico: 'third interception in a little over a week.' Incirlik proximity = extreme Article 5 pressure. Turkey condemnation of Israel strikes + Iran missile threat = caught between two belligerents. Up from 30→32.

india.notes: India monitoring Hormuz crisis — 27 Indian-flagged ships stuck in Persian Gulf. No India-Pakistan friction. GCC drafting UNSC resolution. Stable deterrent.

russia.notes: Trump's 30-day oil sanctions waiver benefits Russia economically. Russia continues arms supply to Iran while calling for ceasefire. FDD: Russia helping Iran with drone tactics. Putin-Trump diplomacy ongoing. No significant change. Stable deterrent.

china.notes: NEW SIGNAL: Largest-ever US arms package proposed for Taiwan (Just Security) — could provoke Beijing response. However: Trump-Xi summit planned Mar 31-Apr 2. NYT: Iran war 'could help China' by draining US weapons stockpiles. Australia sending assets to ME = Indo-Pacific drawdown. China silent on Iran war. China-Japan diplomatic crisis ongoing. Slight uptick 12→13 but still deterrent zone.

north_korea.notes: Kim Jong Un and daughter observed cruise missile tests (Mar 10-12). DPRK condemned US ME attacks. DPRK accused Japan of 'heightening regional security risks' via missile buildup (Reuters/ KCNA). DPRK-China engagement increasing (Kim-Xi correspondence, tourism cooperation). SCMP: Iran-DPRK cooperation revival 'highly likely.' Stable elevated.

russia_ukraine.notes: ISW: continued Russian offensive. 860 Russian casualties in past 24h. Ukraine struck Russian electronics plant + radar stations. Russia scorched-earth on energy grid near collapse (NYT). Zelensky in France seeking support: says US sanctions waiver 'not helping end war.' US postponed three-way settlement talks. Russia earned ~€6bn in 2 weeks from energy crisis. Stable elevated.

pakistan_afghanistan.notes: Entering 3rd week. Overnight strikes reported in Kabul, Kandahar, Paktia. Border fighting ongoing at Spin Boldak/Mohmand. China mediating. Taliban accuses Pakistan of targeting civilians. No major escalation or de-escalation. Stable elevated.

top_signals: Israel strikes near Quds Day rally in Tehran — explosion during state-organized demonstration with thousands present, at least 1 killed (AP/NYT/Al Jazeera/PBS)
Trump: 'US will hit Iran hard over next week' — escalatory rhetoric amid internal White House tug-of-war on exit strategy (Reuters)
Netanyahu confirms Israeli strikes killed top Iranian nuclear scientists — nuclear knowledge base degraded (Al Jazeera/Guardian)
Lebanon death toll rises to 773 killed, 1,933 wounded — 100+ children. Israel destroying bridges, threatening 'Gaza-scale devastation' (Guardian/Al Jazeera)
Hegseth: Khamenei 'wounded and likely hiding.' Total US casualties: 13 killed. Marines upgraded to 2,500 + additional ship (BBC/AP)
Third Iranian ballistic missile intercepted by NATO over Turkey — sirens near Incirlik Air Base (Reuters/Bloomberg/Politico)
Largest-ever US arms package proposed for Taiwan — could provoke China response (Just Security)
Israel destroys bridge in south Lebanon, drops leaflets over Beirut — expanded incursion preparing (Al Jazeera/Bloomberg)

changed_signals.de_escalation_watch: CONTINUED DE-ESCALATION on Iran nuclear track: nuclear scientists killed + Isfahan stockpile buried = two-pronged degradation of nuclear capability. But CONVENTIONAL ESCALATION accelerating: Quds Day rally strike (targeting civilian demonstration area), 13 US dead, 2,500 Marines, Khamenei wounded/hiding, Israel threatening Gaza-scale devastation in Lebanon. White House internally divided on exit. Global probability edges up 39→40.

latest_news.headline: Explosion near Quds Day rally in Tehran — Israel struck state-organized demonstration
latest_news.text: Explosion during annual al-Quds Day rally in Tehran. Israel had warned it would target the area. Thousands present. At least 1 killed. Senior Iranian officials attended. Strikes shaking government-organized event = major propaganda/escalation catalyst.

latest_news.headline: Trump: 'US will hit Iran hard over next week' — White House internal tug-of-war on exit
latest_news.text: Trump says war 'going well,' vows continued escalation. Meanwhile Reuters reports complex internal White House tug-of-war: aides vie to affect outcome, no clear exit strategy. CNN: officials told lawmakers they hadn't planned for Hormuz closure.

latest_news.headline: Netanyahu confirms Israeli strikes killed top Iranian nuclear scientists
latest_news.text: Netanyahu announced several top Iranian nuclear scientists killed in Israeli strikes. Combined with Isfahan stockpile buried, Iran's nuclear program faces both material and human capital destruction.

latest_news.headline: Lebanon death toll: 773 killed, 1,933 wounded — Israel threatens 'Gaza-scale devastation'
latest_news.text: 773 killed in Lebanon (including 100+ children), 1,933 wounded. Israel destroying bridges in south Lebanon, dropped leaflets over Beirut threatening Gaza-scale devastation. Bloomberg: expanded incursion preparing. 800K+ displaced.

latest_news.headline: Hegseth: Khamenei 'wounded and likely hiding' — 13 US service members killed total
latest_news.text: US Defense Secretary Hegseth says Iran's Supreme Leader Khamenei is 'wounded and likely hiding.' Total US military casualties now 13 killed. 2,500 Marines deploying (upgraded from 2,200) plus at least one additional ship.

latest_news.headline: Third Iranian ballistic missile intercepted by NATO over Turkey — sirens near Incirlik
latest_news.text: Third ballistic missile from Iran shot down by NATO air defenses over Turkey in 10 days. Sirens blared in Adana province near Incirlik Air Base. Turkey formally demanding Tehran clarification, warning against further attacks. NATO confirmed intercept.

latest_news.headline: Largest-ever US arms package proposed for Taiwan
latest_news.text: Largest-ever US arms package for Taiwan proposed, suggesting administration intends to sustain/expand military support for Taipei. Meanwhile Iran war draining US weapons stockpiles at 'blistering rate.' Trump-Xi summit planned Mar 31-Apr 2.

latest_news.headline: Zelensky in France: US sanctions waiver 'not helping end war' — talks postponed
latest_news.text: Zelensky visits France, criticizes US decision to ease Russian oil sanctions. US postponed three-way settlement talks. Russia continuing offensive — 860 casualties in past 24h. Ukraine energy grid near collapse. Russia earned ~€6bn from energy crisis.

outlook: CRITICAL — CONVENTIONAL ESCALATION ACCELERATING, NUCLEAR DE-ESCALATION CONTINUES: Day 14 evening brings the most significant civilian-area strike yet: Israel hit near the Quds Day rally in Tehran, where thousands of Iranians gathered for the annual state-organized event. At least 1 killed. This strike — on a civilian demonstration Israel had explicitly warned about — is both a military escalation (striking central Tehran) and a propaganda gift to the Iranian regime. Trump vows to 'hit Iran hard over next week.' Hegseth says Khamenei 'wounded and likely hiding.' Total US dead: 13. Marines upgraded to 2,500. NUCLEAR DE-ESCALATION CONTINUES: Netanyahu confirms strikes killed top Iranian nuclear scientists, adding to Isfahan stockpile burial. Iran's nuclear program facing material + human capital destruction. But conventional trajectory is deeply escalatory. Israel-Lebanon: 773 dead, Gaza-scale threats. Turkey: third missile intercept, Incirlik proximity. White House internally divided — no clear exit. Key watch for next 12h: whether Quds Day strike galvanizes Iranian public resolve or accelerates regime fracture; whether Israel opens expanded Lebanon incursion; whether Turkey moves toward Article 4; whether Trump's 'hard week' promise translates to new target sets.
